feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Elimination of SAP PI PO systems
entry.content:
Hello. I'm creating this post so we can all share our opinions constructively.

I recently learned that SAP plans to eliminate SAP PI/PO systems to focus solely on Integration Suite.

As someone who works extensively with PO, I feel saddened and angry with SAP. After all these years of
working and continuously acquiring knowledge in PO, SAP seems to have decided to kick it to the curb.
All that knowledge feels like throwing it away. Similarly, SAP is forcing clients who have already invested
money in implementing PO to migrate to Integration Suite. I believe many clients don't want the cloud and prefer
to keep their servers on-premises for security or other reasons. However, SAP doesn't care and is forcing all
clients to move their infrastructure to the cloud... and of course, spend money again.
Personally, I find this despicable.

And although I work with PO, I also work with Integration Suite. I find IS awful, much more difficult,
since you have to develop the business logic by creating huge and complicated iFlows, while in PO,
integrations are generally point-to-point, making them easier to implement and understand.
And then there are options like the Monitor, which is rubbish compared to the PO monitor, where it shows
you all the messages, their log traces, payloads... without you having to do anything, by default.
In IS, if you want to see anything in the monitor, you have to develop it yourself to save headers,
log files, payloads, etc., wasting your time.

Anyway, I find it incredible that SAP has played this game with its customers, forcing them to switch.
And of course, it's forcing us, the employees, to learn another way of doing integrations, throwing away
our knowledge. (I just hope SAP doesn't do very well.)

What do you think?
What's your opinion?
Are you for or against it?

Let's see if we can start a thread to discuss this and get it to someone at SAP.

Thanks. Regards.

entry.title: WSSE error "The security token could not be authenticated or authorized" in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello All,
I am currently working on an integration flow where SOAP 1.x adapter with WS-Security configuration is used in combination with a request-reply step. The request execution completes successfully but after the response generation phase the request-reply step fails with the following error - "org.apache.cxf.binding.soap.SoapFault: The security token could not be authenticated or authorized, cause: org.apache.wss4j.common.ext.WSSecurityException: The security token could not be authenticated or authorized"

I would like to understand if some kind of validation is occurring for the response payload at the SAP CPI side and is that why it is failing and that is there any mechanism to bypass this validation? Also would like to get clarity on another thing - in the response payload in the binary security token, valuetype attribute is PKIPathv1, which means the complete certificate chain is bundled together for signing purpose. But in SAP cpi we have uploaded the certificates separately as leaf, intermediate and root. Can this be a cause? 


When trace is enabled, I am able to see the SOAP response payload in the message logs. However, I would like to understand how the response payload can be captured within the exception subprocess when this failure occurs. I tried using ${in.body}, but it provided a blank body.
Has anyone encountered a similar scenario or have suggestions on how to capture the response payload in the exception subprocess?
Kindly see the below attachments for reference.

entry.title: Access CDS View data through SAP Integration Suite for Power BI / Excel without embedding credential
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to expose data from an SAP CDS View through SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) so that it can be consumed from Power BI and Excel. It is compulsory to use Integration Suite.

The main constraint of the scenario is authentication.

The Power BI and Excel files will be shared with multiple users, so I cannot embed credentials (for example in VBA scripts or connection strings).
Instead, the idea is to expose the CDS data through Integration Suite as a controlled endpoint.

The desired architecture is:

 
Power BI / Excel
↓
SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)
↓
SAP Gateway OData Service (generated from CDS View)
 

The CDS View is already published as an OData service in SAP Gateway, and it works correctly when accessed directly.

What I have tried so far

I tried two different approaches in Integration Suite.

1. OData → OData Integration Flow

First I created an integration flow using OData adapters on both sides.

Configuration:

Sender adapter

OData V2
Operation: Query (GET)

Receiver adapter

 

OData V2
Resource path: <EntitySet>
Query options: ${header.CamelHttpQuery}
Authentication: Basic (credential stored in Integration Suite)

Goal: create a transparent OData proxy.

Result:

  • The iFlow deployed successfully.

  • I was able to retrieve the metadata from the CDS view through this approach.

  • PowerBI detected the Set BUT gave Internal Server Error (Error no500), so I could not access the info (same problem if tried in browser).

2. HTTPS Sender → HTTP Receiver proxy

I then attempted a (supposely) simpler HTTP proxy approach.

Sender:

HTTPS
Address:
/powerbi/cds02/*
 
Integration flow:
HTTPS Sender
↓
Start
↓
Content Modifier
↓
HTTP Receiver

Content Modifier headers:

CamelHttpPath = ${header.CamelHttpPath}
CamelHttpQuery = ${header.CamelHttpQuery}
 

The idea was to dynamically forward the request path and query string.
In SAP Cloud Integration, the CamelHttpQuery header contains the query parameters from the original request URL.

Receiver configuration:

Address: https://<backend>/sap/opu/odata/sap/<SERVICE_NAME>
Authentication: Basic

Result:

  • The iFlow deploys successfully.

  • However every request returns: An internal server error occured: The MPL ID for the failed message is : (number)

So in practice this HTTP proxy approach does not reach the CDS data at all.

Current status

Summary of what I achieved so far:

Approach Result
OData → ODataMetadata reached (column names got), but not data in it (internal server error)
HTTPS → HTTP proxyRequests fail with internal server error

Questions

  1. What is the recommended architecture to expose CDS data through Integration Suite for consumption by Power BI / Excel?

  2. Is using Integration Suite as an OData proxy the correct approach in this scenario?

  3. Are there special considerations for handling $metadata and query parameters when forwarding OData requests through CPI?

  4. Is there a better approach to solve the authentication constraint (no embedded credentials in Excel / Power BI files)?

Any guidance or examples of similar implementations would be greatly appreciated. I could not even find any tutorials or anything (involving Integration Suite), though I guess it is something used in many companies (it is my first time doing this).

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing Integration Capability with SAP EWM 9.5
entry.content: Standard SAP DM integration with EWM is capable of supporting the minimum version of SAP S/4HANA 2021 and above (both embedded EWM and Decentralized EWM on S/4HANA). Is there any note to refer to that SAP DM supports integration with EWM 9.5?

entry.title: Is it possible to configure a Static IP for the EDI Exchange in the CPI?
entry.content:
Hi everybody,
anyone has an idea, whether it is possible get a static IP for sending EDI-Message in the CPI?
Background of the Question is the fact that we are exchanging EDIFACT Message with Partners, which have a quite strict Firewall. And if our IP-Adress or IP-Adress-Range isn’t whitelisted on there side, our Message are getting rejected.

Our goal is to send EDIFACT messages like an invoice or an order response via the CPI with an AS2 Connector with a static IP-Adress or static IP-Adress-Range.

Thanks
Tobias

entry.title: Transfer objects created in Integration Suite to Cloud Integration NEO. (Message-type and data-type)
entry.content:
Hi everyone !

    I'm working on a project migrating SAP PI/PO to Cloud Integration NEO (the migration from NEO to Cloud Foundry will be next year). To perform the migration using the migration tool, following the instructions provided by SAP, we created a BTP Trial account and imported all the objects from PI/PO. After the migration, I'm transferring the objects and iFlows to BTP NEO (Cloud Integration). However, Cloud Integration NEO doesn't have the option to import Data-Types or Message-Types that were created during the migration in the Integration Suite of the Trial account. My question is: has anyone done this transfer from Integration Suite to Cloud Integration NEO before? How do I transfer the data-types and message-types to NEO? And if I have to create them manually in NEO, how do I create these objects in their iFlows on Cloud Integration NEO ?

entry.title: SAP CPI - json value characater gets escaped when convert to xml
entry.content:
I have a converter which convert json to xml. 

In my json I have a field which has the '&' character  in the value, like this: 

"nextPageLink":"/records?pageNumber=2&pageSize=100"

after CPI Json to xml convert it to xml, the value of the nextPageLink was converted as:

records?pageNumber=2&pageSize=100.

The '&'  is escaped as & , how can I avoid this?

entry.title: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - SAP S4 to Biztalk 2016
entry.content:
Hello Team,

While Connecting from SAP S4 Cloud to MS Biztalk 2016 it is failing with below error. Totally stucked up with this error for few weeks. SAP states to uplift SAP NCO connector to 3.1.6 it doesn't really help.

 

Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - Stucked in SM58

Latest Update from SAP- Really issue is strange and fishy!!!

Logs:

 

MS REPLY:

 

 

 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: Handling large file transfers (1GB+) in BTP-Integration Suite !
entry.content:
We are currently evaluating the migration of high-volume 'File-to-File' pass-through scenarios (exceeding 1GB) from SAP PI/PO to SAP BTP Integration Suite. While this is currently stable in our legacy environment, we need to ensure the Cloud Integration architecture can handle similar loads without memory bottlenecks.
when we try the same requirement in CPI ,it’s getting failed to read the file .

Any recommendations would be appreciated,thank you

-regards ,Bhaskar

Cloud Integration

entry.title: Connection to IXOS Server from CPI - Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My requirement is to store the PDF files into IXOS server which is an opentext server for archiving the files and once we send any file we will get a archive id and it should be updated in S4HANA . Could anyone provide inputs on how to establish connection to IXOS server i mean how to store files in IXOS server from CPI whether it is API based push or SFTP based push. 

 

Regards,

Pavan Kalyan Racha

entry.title: Provide values for node created with CreateIf in CPI
entry.content:
Hi,

I've some rather confusing issue in CPI.

I'm creating a node "ContactComms" via createIf based on some conditions. I have 7 values in the queue for the createIf, one is "true" and is resulting in a node as expected.

Within node "ContactComms", there is a tag "Value". Although number of values provided in the queue for this is the same, I never get a value in "Value".

The result has no tag "value". I would expect one with mail ab-email@gmail.com

I am happy for any hints on this issues. Thank you very much!

Harry

 

EDIT: As I wrote, I came to the conclusion. For value, "splitByValue" is the right function to use instead of "removeContext"

entry.title: Exploring Middleware Options for SAP Public Cloud to OFTP2 Integration
entry.content:
HI SAP Friends.

Exploring Middleware Options for SAP Public Cloud to OFTP2 Integration

In one of our current engagements with a Europe-based client, we are managing a business scenario involving approximately 35 interfaces. These interfaces are responsible for integrating the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system with an OFTP2 adapter, currently facilitated through SEEBURGER.

As part of our evaluation, we are exploring alternative solutions—either within the SAP ecosystem or through third-party applications—that can provide similar OFTP2 communication capabilities as SEEBURGER. The objective is to identify a scalable, secure, and cost-effective middleware solution that can efficiently handle multiple interfaces while ensuring compliance with OFTP2 standards.

We are particularly interested in solutions that offer:

  • Seamless integration with SAP Public Cloud
  • Native or supported OFTP2 capabilities
  • Strong monitoring and error-handling features
  • Flexibility to scale across multiple interfaces

If you have experience with similar integration scenarios or can recommend suitable alternatives, your insights would be highly valuable.

entry.title: CPI SOAP error - HTTP response '400: Bad Request'
entry.content:
Hi,

When an external SOAP service is called via SOAP UI with proper request payload, we get expected response, but when we use the same request payload to call the same Service via SAP CPI, I get the below error

org.apache.cxf.interceptor.Fault: Could not send Message., cause: org.apache.cxf.transport.http.HTTPException: HTTP response '400: Bad Request. Bad Request' when communicating with URL

It was working fine until 06/03/2026. The SOAP vendor doesn't update anything, so I suspect SAP has changed something.

Has anyone got this error before?

Regards,

Tri

entry.title: Failed to fetch oAuth token from IFlow
entry.content:
Hi CPI Experts,

I am trying to fetch a set of data using IFlow from an api which is publically available and secured with oAuth.
I have created a security material with the following details:

Then I am using this in the IFlow as 

 

When I trigger the IFlow, fetching auth token fails with the error:

com.google.common.util.concurrent.UncheckedExecutionException: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Exception occured while fetching OAuth Token. Reason: mysite.com: Name or service not known, cause: java.net.UnknownHostException: mysite.com: Name or service not known

 

I have tested the API and credential from Bruno and it is working, which indicates that there is no issue with the application server.

Any inputs are much appreciated.
Thanks.

entry.title: Strengthen Kafka integration
entry.content:
Hello community!

For an international customer, I’m looking for insights to strengthen the integration with Kafka, in an EDA context.

I’m specifically looking for these topics

  • Private Link Architecture. B2B VPN tunnel, Cloud Connector, … ?
  • Multiple event types on a single topic
  • Better visibility on errors, especially when multiple schema rules are violated
  • Offset commit mechanism
  • AVRO schema support
  • Retry behavior and flexible strategy

Yes, I know there is Advanced Event Mesh. But the choice for Kafka was made a long time ago. Currently, we do not use the native Kafka adapter within the Integration Suite.

I’m thus looking for insights within the Integration Suite context to strengthen our current integration. To make it more robust, safe and futureproof.

Looking forward to your feedback!

Thanks.
Dimitri

entry.title: integration of SAP Public Cloud with AMAZON
entry.content:
Dear Expert, 

Can you explain the steps for Integrating SAP public Cloud and AMAZON for flowing of Sales and Stock Data . 

Thanks & Regards  

Tapas

entry.title: Integration btw SAP SuccessFactors to SAP Ariba for user master data flow ..
entry.content:
need to Integrate  Success factors to SAP Ariba for  User master data integration as if the user is created in SF that can automatically be created in Ariba....what architecture we are thinking is  

Success Factors->SFTP->CPI->CIG->Ariba Buying/Sourcing.

Can anyone suggest will this work or not and if there is any other approach for the same requirement. and what actions mapping will be required from CIG, Ariba end for the requires integration.

entry.title: Unable to set property from an XML using the XPath.
entry.content:
Hello Experts.

I am unable to set a property using an XPATH from the XML Payload.

Following is the XML payload.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" standalone="yes"?>
<entry xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"
xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata"
xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices"
xml:base="https://api41preview.sapsf.com/odata/v2/">
<content type="application/xml">hello</content>
<d:fileExtension>txt</d:fileExtension>
</entry>

Note that I have simplified this payload eliminating all the other nodes so as to focus on the question at hand. 

Following is the sample I-flow I created to explain the issue.

In Content Modifier 1 I have hardcoded the body with the Payload

In Content Modifier 2 I am setting the Property using the Xpath as follows

Action Name Type Data Type Value

ActionNameTypeData TypeValue

Create

fileExtension

XPath

java.lang.String

/entry/d:fileExtension/text()

In Content Modifier 3 I am checking if the Property is set. This is where I find that the property is not getting set.

I have confirmed that the XPATH is correct testing it wit other tools.

I have also included the namespaces d and m on the Runtime Configuration Tab on the Integration Flow as follows

entry.title: Unable to modify API URL after completing RTI Connectivity configuration (CIAIS scenario)
entry.content:
I have completed all configuration steps for CIAIS following the scenario “Configuring RTI Connectivity in SAP IBP”.

During the setup of the communication with SAP IBP, the API URL field was configured incorrectly.

Incorrect URL entered:
https://my308167.scmibp1.ondemand.com

Correct URL should be:
https://my308167-api.scmibp1.ondemand.com

After completing the configuration, the task was marked as Completed, and I am currently unable to reopen or edit the step to correct the API URL.

Additionally, all subsequent steps in the scenario were executed using this incorrect configuration

entry.title: Establish Connection between SAP SF and 3rd Party SFTP Host
entry.content:
SAP SuccessFactors SFTP based Integration with 3rd Party System using SFTP Non-SuccessFactors SFTP Host

1. Introduction

This document describes the process of integrating SAP SuccessFactors (SF) with a 3rd party system using SFTP-based file transfer via Integration Center. Unlike the standard SF internal SFTP, this integration leverages the 3rd party’s non-SF hosted SFTP account, requiring additional steps for connectivity, authentication, and whitelisting. 

2. Problem Statement

SAP SuccessFactors (SF) typically supports file transfers using its internal SFTP server. However, in this case, the integration requires connecting to a 3rd party’s external SFTP host. This introduces challenges such as:

  • Establishing secure connectivity between SF and the external host.
  • Managing mutual whitelisting of IP addresses.
  • Handling encryption requirements with PGP keys.
  • Coordinating responsibilities across three stakeholders: 3rd Party System, SuccessFactors Team, and SAP Support.

This document outlines the integration process, divided into sub-processes, with commentary to highlight key considerations.

3. Process to establish connection

3.1 Overview

 

3.2 Requirement Gathering

Activities:

  • Collect SFTP host details (address, port, file path, user credentials).
  • Obtain host IP addresses.
  • Confirm user permissions for file path access.
  • Receive PGP encryption key from the 3rd party.

Commentary:
This step ensures all technical prerequisites are captured before configuration begins. Missing or incorrect details at this stage can delay integration or cause connectivity failures.

 

3.3 Authentication Setup

Activities:

  • Generate SSH key pair in SuccessFactors.
  • Share the public key with the 3rd party for authentication.
  • Provide the SAP SF datacentre IP to the 3rd party for whitelisting.

Steps:

Commentary:
Authentication is critical for secure file transfer. Using SSH keys avoids password-based vulnerabilities, while whitelisting ensures only trusted IPs can connect.

  • Category: Authentication Key (SSH)
  • Key Size: As per requirements of 3rd Party
  • Valid until: NA (For no expiry)
  • For SAP SF IP Address: 2089448 - SuccessFactors Data Center Name, Location, URL, Domain Name, Mail Server details, SFTP details and Outbound IP addresses

 

3.4 SAP Support Coordination

Activities:

  • Raise a ticket with SAP Support.
  • Request whitelisting of the 3rd party’s SFTP host IP and URL.
  • Obtain confirmation once whitelisting is complete.

Steps:

Commentary:
This step requires coordination with SAP Support. Without whitelisting, SuccessFactors cannot establish outbound connections to the external SFTP host.

 

3.5 Integration Center Configuration

Activities:

  • Configure outbound file transfer job in Integration Center.
  • Apply PGP encryption using the provided key.

Steps:

Commentary:
Integration Center acts as the execution engine for file transfers. Proper configuration ensures files are securely encrypted and delivered to the correct directory.

  • SFTP Detail (Server Host Address, Username, File Details): To be provided by 3rd party
  • Authentication Key: Created at Security Center
  • File Encryption: Imported at Security Center

 

3.6 Validation & Testing

Activities:

  • Perform test transfers to the external SFTP host.
  • Validate encryption, permissions, and file delivery.
  • Confirm successful end-to-end integration.

Commentary:
Testing verifies that all components—network, authentication, encryption, and file transfer—work together seamlessly. This step reduces risk before moving to production.

4. Roles and Responsibilities

Stakeholder

Responsibilities

3rd Party

Provide SFTP details, IP addresses, user permissions, PGP key and add SSH Key

SF Admin

Configure Integration Center, generate SSH key, apply encryption and testing

SAP Support

Whitelist 3rd party SFTP host IP & URL
